title: Mustardy Chinese Custard Bao
description: A dense, festive Chinese dessert featuring custard, coconut-cream-crack, and a hint of mustard, inspired by Chaozhou cuisine.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C) and line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
In a medium bowl, whisk together the flour, warm water, and vegetable oil to create a dough.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 4 equal pieces and roll each piece into a ball.
Flatten each ball into a disk and place a tablespoon of granulated sugar in the center of each disk.
Fold the dough over the sugar to form a ball and seal the edges by pinching them together.
Place the bao on the prepared baking sheet and brush with a little bit of water.
Bake the bao in the preheated oven for 20 minutes, or until golden brown.
While the bao are baking, make the custard by whisking together the eggs, sugar, coconut milk, and vanilla extract in a medium bowl.
Add the baking powder to the custard and whisk until well combined.
Remove the bao from the oven and let them cool for 5 minutes.
Split the bao in half and fill each half with a generous amount of custard.
Top the custard with a sprinkle of chopped scallions and a drizzle of Dijon mustard.
Serve the Mustardy Chinese Custard Bao warm, garnished with additional scallions if desired.
